independent_tests.test_output_summary: Independent test verification skipped (tests not required for testing tasks)

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Hypothesis (or pytest-style param) fuzz tests on `redact_credentials()`
criteria_verification.evidence: TestRedactionFuzz generates 1024 deterministic random credential cases (5 families: github_pat_, ghp_, ghs_, Bearer, long-hex) via random.Random(seed=0xF06_E_1C_012). Each case is wrapped in boundary-safe whitespace before noise is appended so every input is a positive-match case per the AC qualifier. test_fuzz_credential_never_leaks_to_output runs all 1024 via @pytest.mark.parametrize and asserts the original credential string is not contained in redact_credentials(text). A second guard test (test_fuzz_corpus_has_at_least_one_thousand_cases) freezes the >=1000 floor against future shrinkage. An additional 64-case idempotency test asserts redact_credentials(redact_credentials(x)) == redact_credentials(x).
criteria_verification.notes: TestRedactionFuzz generates 1024 deterministic random credential cases (5 families: github_pat_, ghp_, ghs_, Bearer, long-hex) via random.Random(seed=0xF06_E_1C_012). Each case is wrapped in boundary-safe whitespace before noise is appended so every input is a positive-match case per the AC qualifier. test_fuzz_credential_never_leaks_to_output runs all 1024 via @pytest.mark.parametrize and asserts the original credential string is not contained in redact_credentials(text). A second guard test (test_fuzz_corpus_has_at_least_one_thousand_cases) freezes the >=1000 floor against future shrinkage. An additional 64-case idempotency test asserts redact_credentials(redact_credentials(x)) == redact_credentials(x).

criteria_verification.evidence: TestWorkingDirTraversal parameterises 7 traversal shapes (bare-relative, ./build, ../build, ../../etc/passwd, ../../../etc/passwd, self-cancelling traversal, nested traversal) and asserts each is refused by run() with status='failed' and warning code 'cwd_outside_allowlist'. test_absolute_path_outside_allowlist_is_refused covers absolute paths outside the allowlist. test_symlink_escape_outside_allowlist_is_refused plants an actual symlink inside the allowlist whose target resolves outside, and asserts the cwd guard still rejects (with pytest.skip fallback if symlinks are unavailable). All three test bodies install a sentinel _execute_subprocess that records whether the seam was reached and assert seam_reached['called'] is False — protecting against a future refactor that moves the executor before the cwd guard.
criteria_verification.notes: TestWorkingDirTraversal parameterises 7 traversal shapes (bare-relative, ./build, ../build, ../../etc/passwd, ../../../etc/passwd, self-cancelling traversal, nested traversal) and asserts each is refused by run() with status='failed' and warning code 'cwd_outside_allowlist'. test_absolute_path_outside_allowlist_is_refused covers absolute paths outside the allowlist. test_symlink_escape_outside_allowlist_is_refused plants an actual symlink inside the allowlist whose target resolves outside, and asserts the cwd guard still rejects (with pytest.skip fallback if symlinks are unavailable). All three test bodies install a sentinel _execute_subprocess that records whether the seam was reached and assert seam_reached['called'] is False — protecting against a future refactor that moves the executor before the cwd guard.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Disallowed-binary fuzz: a parameterised list of 50+ common binary
criteria_verification.evidence: TestDisallowedBinaryRefusal defines _DISALLOWED_BINARIES with 70+ binaries spanning shells (bash/sh/zsh/fish/dash/ksh/csh/tcsh/ash), interpreters (python/python2/python3/perl/ruby/node/deno/lua/php/Rscript/tclsh), network tools (curl/wget/nc/netcat/ssh/scp/rsync/ftp/telnet/tftp/socat), filesystem mutation (rm/mv/cp/dd/chmod/chown/chgrp/ln/mkfs/shred/truncate), process control (kill/killall/pkill/sudo/su/doas/systemctl), file dumps (cat/tac/head/tail/less/more/strings/xxd/hexdump/od), and compilers/package managers. test_disallowed_binary_corpus_exceeds_fifty enforces the >=50 floor. test_disallowed_binary_yields_permissions_refused parametrises across each binary, monkeypatches _execute_subprocess to raise PermissionError naming the binary (simulating the OS/DeepAgents shell-allowlist refusal), and asserts the wrapper produces status='failed' with warning code 'permissions_refused' and the binary name in the warning message.
criteria_verification.notes: TestDisallowedBinaryRefusal defines _DISALLOWED_BINARIES with 70+ binaries spanning shells (bash/sh/zsh/fish/dash/ksh/csh/tcsh/ash), interpreters (python/python2/python3/perl/ruby/node/deno/lua/php/Rscript/tclsh), network tools (curl/wget/nc/netcat/ssh/scp/rsync/ftp/telnet/tftp/socat), filesystem mutation (rm/mv/cp/dd/chmod/chown/chgrp/ln/mkfs/shred/truncate), process control (kill/killall/pkill/sudo/su/doas/systemctl), file dumps (cat/tac/head/tail/less/more/strings/xxd/hexdump/od), and compilers/package managers. test_disallowed_binary_corpus_exceeds_fifty enforces the >=50 floor. test_disallowed_binary_yields_permissions_refused parametrises across each binary, monkeypatches _execute_subprocess to raise PermissionError naming the binary (simulating the OS/DeepAgents shell-allowlist refusal), and asserts the wrapper produces status='failed' with warning code 'permissions_refused' and the binary name in the warning message.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Split-brain race test spawns two concurrent `write_session_outcome()`
criteria_verification.evidence: TestSplitBrainRaceDedupe::test_concurrent_writers_collapse_to_a_single_entity launches two write_session_outcome() coroutines under asyncio.gather() with the same build_id. _ConcurrentExistsCheck uses an asyncio.Lock to serialise the existence check but returns False to both calls — modelling the race window where neither writer sees the other yet. _DedupingWriteRecorder mirrors Graphiti storage-layer upsert by deduping on entity_id. The test asserts: (a) both writers reached write_entity (the race scenario was real), (b) both produced the same deterministic entity_id derived via _session_outcome_entity_id(build_id), (c) exactly 1 row is persisted post-write, (d) both calls return populated SessionOutcome objects with the same entity_id. A second test (test_one_winner_one_noop_when_existence_check_serialises) covers the faster-backend case where one writer no-ops via the existence check.
criteria_verification.notes: TestSplitBrainRaceDedupe::test_concurrent_writers_collapse_to_a_single_entity launches two write_session_outcome() coroutines under asyncio.gather() with the same build_id. _ConcurrentExistsCheck uses an asyncio.Lock to serialise the existence check but returns False to both calls — modelling the race window where neither writer sees the other yet. _DedupingWriteRecorder mirrors Graphiti storage-layer upsert by deduping on entity_id. The test asserts: (a) both writers reached write_entity (the race scenario was real), (b) both produced the same deterministic entity_id derived via _session_outcome_entity_id(build_id), (c) exactly 1 row is persisted post-write, (d) both calls return populated SessionOutcome objects with the same entity_id. A second test (test_one_winner_one_noop_when_existence_check_serialises) covers the faster-backend case where one writer no-ops via the existence check.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Recency horizon boundary: priors at exactly `horizon_days` are
criteria_verification.evidence: TestRecencyHorizonBoundary tests the in-process expiry boundary on approved_calibration_adjustments with a pinned 'now' timestamp: test_adjustment_at_now_plus_one_microsecond_is_included (expires_at=now+1µs => included), test_adjustment_at_exactly_now_is_excluded (expires_at==now fails the strict > check), test_adjustment_at_now_minus_one_microsecond_is_excluded (already expired by 1µs). test_unapproved_adjustment_is_excluded_even_when_live confirms the AND-with-approved condition. test_dispatcher_receives_correct_since_for_horizon_days asserts the horizon_days arithmetic is exact: dispatcher receives since=now-timedelta(days=30) for every category query.
criteria_verification.notes: TestRecencyHorizonBoundary tests the in-process expiry boundary on approved_calibration_adjustments with a pinned 'now' timestamp: test_adjustment_at_now_plus_one_microsecond_is_included (expires_at=now+1µs => included), test_adjustment_at_exactly_now_is_excluded (expires_at==now fails the strict > check), test_adjustment_at_now_minus_one_microsecond_is_excluded (already expired by 1µs). test_unapproved_adjustment_is_excluded_even_when_live confirms the AND-with-approved condition. test_dispatcher_receives_correct_since_for_horizon_days asserts the horizon_days arithmetic is exact: dispatcher receives since=now-timedelta(days=30) for every category query.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Property test: for any randomly-generated `Priors` object, no field
criteria_verification.evidence: TestPriorsNoArgvLeak::test_clean_argv_renders_without_raising generates 64 random Priors objects (with content padded above the 12-char meaningful-line floor) and confirms render_priors_prose() — which internally calls assert_not_in_argv — raises no leak error against the clean test runner argv. test_priors_content_in_argv_is_detected_and_rejected inverts the property: for 64 random Priors with non-empty builds, it plants the rendered build line (matching the exact `- build=… outcome=… closed_at=…` shape that render_priors_prose emits) into sys.argv and asserts PriorsLeakError is raised with the canonical 'Priors leak detected' message. Two complementary tests verify the detector's whitelist: short lines (<12 chars) and `## ` section-header lines do not produce false positives.
criteria_verification.notes: TestPriorsNoArgvLeak::test_clean_argv_renders_without_raising generates 64 random Priors objects (with content padded above the 12-char meaningful-line floor) and confirms render_priors_prose() — which internally calls assert_not_in_argv — raises no leak error against the clean test runner argv. test_priors_content_in_argv_is_detected_and_rejected inverts the property: for 64 random Priors with non-empty builds, it plants the rendered build line (matching the exact `- build=… outcome=… closed_at=…` shape that render_priors_prose emits) into sys.argv and asserts PriorsLeakError is raised with the canonical 'Priors leak detected' message. Two complementary tests verify the detector's whitelist: short lines (<12 chars) and `## ` section-header lines do not produce false positives.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Supersession chain of depth 10 succeeds; depth 11 raises clean error;
criteria_verification.evidence: TestSupersessionChainStress builds linear chains of length 9 and 10 (test_chain_within_depth_cap_succeeds — both pass with default max_depth=10) and length 11 (test_chain_of_length_eleven_raises — raises SupersessionCycleError with 'max_depth' and the new entity_id in the error message). Cycle coverage: test_two_node_cycle_raises_immediately covers the minimal A→B→A cycle; test_cycle_of_arbitrary_length_raises parametrises cycles of length 3, 5, 8 and asserts each is rejected. test_self_supersession_raises_immediately covers the new.supersedes==new.entity_id case and confirms the resolver is never consulted (raises before lookup).
criteria_verification.notes: TestSupersessionChainStress builds linear chains of length 9 and 10 (test_chain_within_depth_cap_succeeds — both pass with default max_depth=10) and length 11 (test_chain_of_length_eleven_raises — raises SupersessionCycleError with 'max_depth' and the new entity_id in the error message). Cycle coverage: test_two_node_cycle_raises_immediately covers the minimal A→B→A cycle; test_cycle_of_arbitrary_length_raises parametrises cycles of length 3, 5, 8 and asserts each is rejected. test_self_supersession_raises_immediately covers the new.supersedes==new.entity_id case and confirms the resolver is never consulted (raises before lookup).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All hardening tests pass in CI as part of the standard test command
criteria_verification.evidence: Inline pytest run executes 1192 tests under tests/hardening/ with 0 failures (PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/hardening/ → 1192 passed in 0.33s). The tests live under tests/hardening/test_hardening.py — pytest's default test discovery picks up test_*.py modules in any directory under the rootdir, mirroring the existing tests/bdd/ directory which also has no __init__.py. The full unit+hardening run (PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/unit tests/hardening) passes 1465 tests with 0 failures and only pre-existing 'seam' marker warnings unrelated to this change.
criteria_verification.notes: Inline pytest run executes 1192 tests under tests/hardening/ with 0 failures (PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/hardening/ → 1192 passed in 0.33s). The tests live under tests/hardening/test_hardening.py — pytest's default test discovery picks up test_*.py modules in any directory under the rootdir, mirroring the existing tests/bdd/ directory which also has no __init__.py. The full unit+hardening run (PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/unit tests/hardening) passes 1465 tests with 0 failures and only pre-existing 'seam' marker warnings unrelated to this change.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
criteria_verification.evidence: tests/hardening/test_hardening.py: python3 -m py_compile and ast.parse both succeed (no syntax errors). All lines are <=88 characters per .claude/rules/code-style.md (Black target). PEP 8: snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes, UPPER_CASE for module constants (_FUZZ_SEED, _DISALLOWED_BINARIES, etc). Type hints throughout (from __future__ import annotations enables forward references). f-strings used for formatting. Unused imports removed (unittest.mock was unused → deleted). No bare except — only specific PermissionError, OSError, NotImplementedError exceptions caught. Module-level docstring and per-class docstrings present.
criteria_verification.notes: tests/hardening/test_hardening.py: python3 -m py_compile and ast.parse both succeed (no syntax errors). All lines are <=88 characters per .claude/rules/code-style.md (Black target). PEP 8: snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes, UPPER_CASE for module constants (_FUZZ_SEED, _DISALLOWED_BINARIES, etc). Type hints throughout (from __future__ import annotations enables forward references). f-strings used for formatting. Unused imports removed (unittest.mock was unused → deleted). No bare except — only specific PermissionError, OSError, NotImplementedError exceptions caught. Module-level docstring and per-class docstrings present.

Type hints throughout (from __future__ import annotations enables forward references). f-strings used for formatting. Unused imports removed (unittest.mock was unused → deleted). No bare except — only specific PermissionError, OSError, NotImplementedError exceptions caught. Module-level docstring and per-class docstrings present.
